title: daucus carota subsp. siculus
intro: Daucus carota subsp. siculus, also known as wild carrot or Mediterranean carrot, is a subspecies of the wild carrot plant (Daucus carota) that is native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antispasmodic, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Daucus carota subsp. siculus include relief from menstrual cramps, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, as well as its ability to reduce fever and improve skin conditions. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in food as a spice or garnish.
